problem.fact: Fresh fixed-rule object-relation arbitration fails promotion on route-specific rows after action-time decision rows are written.
problem.failure_mechanism: Object visibility, candidate-specific own-view support, and relation-depth consistency do not establish ObjectNav goal validity in repeated-object scenes.
problem.agent_inference: The next method component should be a branch router that requests different nonterminal evidence for distinct failure mechanisms, not a threshold change on the failed arbitration rule.

design_scope.purpose: Convert the fresh arbitration failure taxonomy into branch-specific nonterminal evidence requests before any terminal utility contract.
design_scope.allowed_label_use: Evaluation labels may be used only to audit branch coverage after action-time branch rows are written.
allowed_action_inputs: semantic rank and score from non-GT candidate artifact
relation-depth status and relation-depth support counts
candidate association counts from action-time detector/SAM2 rows
candidate geometry and non-GT standoff observation metadata
non-GT source-top, rival, and context-candidate roles

action_time_trigger: one candidate has independent candidate-specific own-view support
that candidate has relation-depth-resolved or high relation-depth evidence
competing candidates remain semantically plausible or relation-depth plausible
required_evidence: contrastive goal-region evidence between unique-support candidate and plausible rivals
multi-view object-instance consistency across source, support, and relation-anchor views
goal-region occupancy or route-context evidence that can disconfirm visible-object false positives

action_time_trigger: one or more relation-depth-plausible candidates lack strong own-view support
another visible candidate dominates own-view support
candidate set contains unresolved repeated-object rivals
required_evidence: candidate-centered own-view recheck for low-own-support plausible candidates
standoff view from the candidate side rather than the visible rival side
explicit report of whether missing own-view support is observation failure or true absence

action_time_trigger: relation-depth status is partial for one or more plausible candidates
candidate has base support or semantic plausibility but relation-depth evidence is incomplete
candidate cannot be safely rejected by missing relation-depth resolution alone
required_evidence: additional relation-anchor view with explicit out-of-FOV/depth-mismatch accounting
relation-depth completion evidence before reject or provisional status
separate report for observation incompleteness versus candidate invalidity

action_time_trigger: candidate has no independent own-view support
candidate relation-depth evidence is missing or unresolved
no competing branch gives the candidate positive goal-validity evidence
required_evidence: guarded negative accounting for unsupported candidates
branch coverage report proving this guard does not hide positives
defer-or-recheck rule when positive plausibility evidence exists

required_diagnostic_tasks.name: branch_router_rows
required_diagnostic_tasks.purpose: Write action-time request and candidate rows assigning every fresh failure request to one or more evidence branches before labels are joined.

required_diagnostic_tasks.name: branch_coverage_audit
required_diagnostic_tasks.purpose: Join the existing evaluation-only failure taxonomy after branch rows are written and report which branch covers each observed failure tag.

required_diagnostic_tasks.name: next_evidence_contract_selection
required_diagnostic_tasks.purpose: Select the first branch-specific observation/evidence implementation without allowing terminal commits.

interpretation_rule.fact: The contract is derived from Docker-verified fresh failure diagnosis and contains no terminal commit policy.
interpretation_rule.paper_claim: No paper claim is allowed from this design contract alone.
interpretation_rule.agent_inference: The most paper-relevant next branch is unique-support visibility not being goal validity, because it explains why a seemingly strong visible-object signal can still produce wrong ObjectNav goals.
interpretation_rule.user_decision_needed: None for the router contract. User judgment may be needed later if goal-region validity should be promoted as a separate metric.
